children.text: Today, on my father-in-law's birthday, I begin to author this master document for the Income Insurance program.  What it intends to do is provide the framework for people to be able to access the intellectual resources to comprehend the flow of the economy and never make a foul move — to always know that they will be able to find what they need — and to have that sense of providence properly represented in financial ledgers.

Someone somewhere has to have some idea how to produce something that will satisfy us to see as our first chapter.  That person could be Jeremiah, he is not sure.  So.  He's never written a book.  A stranger would probably look at this and say, "Look at this floundering fellow.  This is a website called Crousia, but the main page is titled Income Insurance.  What is he even doing?"  An AI would probably read this and say, "Here, change this to that, and that to this," and we still wouldn't feel good about any of it.

But if we have collaborators, each paying $5 a month for the opportunity to participate in something deep, meaningful, and perhaps even far-reaching, then perhaps we will all feel like something is worthwhile which is going forward into the abyss.  The trouble is if we keep it all in first-person, then people will be putting words into Jeremiah's mouth, which isn't the sort of collaboration we are supposed to be experiencing.  Thus, we have switched out all of the pronouns as appropriate.
